name: training_pipeline_v1
description: Meta-dataset: adapterOS training pipeline knowledge — dataset validation, configuration selection, loss curve interpretation, and adapter packaging/provenance.

entries.notes: 400 examples covering four subcategories: dataset_validation (101), config_selection (98), loss_curve_interpretation (97), adapter_packaging (104). Synthetic, curated examples designed to teach the model accurate knowledge of the adapterOS training pipeline phases, configuration selection heuristics, loss diagnostics, and .aos bundle provenance mechanics.

provenance.review_notes: First version. Covers all five pipeline phases (dataset_build, preprocess, train_validation_split, training_loop, validation_early_stopping) and the .aos bundle lifecycle. All examples target quality >= 0.90. Subcategory distribution is intentionally near-equal (~25% each) for balanced adapter training.

evaluation_gates: Model correctly identifies invalid JSONL dataset conditions (missing fields, type mismatches, empty values)
Model recommends appropriate rank/alpha/lr/epochs for given dataset size and domain
Model correctly diagnoses overfitting, underfitting, gradient explosion, and good convergence from loss curve descriptions
Model accurately describes .aos bundle contents, provenance chain, and BLAKE3 integrity verification
Model explains the purpose and sequencing of all five pipeline phases
Model identifies correct early stopping behavior given patience and validation loss sequences
intent: Train the adapterOS model to understand, explain, and reason about its own training pipeline — enabling it to assist operators in diagnosing training issues, selecting configurations, interpreting results, and understanding adapter provenance.
